pressItems.title: The Community Spotlight Series: Featuring The School of Freedom Project
pressItems.description: A live, podcast-style community conversation highlighting The School of Freedom’s mission, impact, and the transformative power of education for Afghan girls and refugee students.

pressItems.title: CPR Training Event Recap
pressItems.description: We are proud to share a recap from our recent CPR training event. Thank you to everyone who participated and became CPR certified. These life-saving skills play a critical role in protecting and supporting our communities. We are grateful for the opportunity to collaborate with dedicated student organizations and individuals committed to making a difference. We look forward to continuing this work and hosting more impactful events in the future.

pressItems.title: Transforming Education for Afghan Girls: A Conversation with the School of Freedom
pressItems.description: In this English-language interview, Jasmin Kouhkan, MSW and Refugee Student Wellness Advisor at CIRH, speaks with Moheb Salemi, Program Manager and Founding Member of the School of Freedom, about how the initiative is reshaping education for Afghan girls and refugee youth. The discussion highlights the School of Freedom’s mission to provide accessible, culturally responsive learning opportunities for girls banned from formal education under Taliban rule, as well as newcomer students in the United States. Through remote learning, after-school tutoring, mentorship, and academic advising, the program emphasizes dignity, safety, and opportunity, empowering students to reclaim their right to learn. The video includes both a presentation and a Q&A session.

pressItems.title: Inside the School of Freedom: Empowering Afghan Girls and Refugee Youth
pressItems.description: In this Dari-language interview, Rafiullah Sadiq, MD and Refugee Health Navigator at CIRH, speaks with Najiullah Walid Jailani, Founder and President of the School of Freedom, about how the organization is transforming education for Afghan girls and refugee youth. The conversation highlights the program’s mission to provide accessible, culturally responsive learning opportunities for girls banned from schooling under Taliban rule and for newcomer students in the United States. Featuring both a presentation and Q&A session, the video explores the School of Freedom’s remote learning model, after-school tutoring, community mentorship, and commitment to dignity, safety, and opportunity.

pressItems.title: Watch: School of Freedom on YouTube
pressItems.description: Watch our interview and story about how The School of Freedom is empowering Afghan girls and refugee youth.

pressItems.title: UC Davis Alum Creates Underground Education Pipeline for Afghan Girls
pressItems.description: CapRadio profiles how Walid Jailani and The School of Freedom rapidly scaled remote education for Afghan girls—over 1,200 served, safety protections in place, and a curriculum spanning English, math, science, and art.

pressItems.title: Student-led school empowers 1,200 Afghan girls
pressItems.description: Podcast interview discussing the impact and growth of The School of Freedom program.

pressItems.title: Walid Jailani Represents UC Davis at UC Regents UC Inspires Series
pressItems.description: Walid Jailani, founder of the School of Freedom, had the honor of representing UC Davis at the UC Board of Regents UC Inspires series, sharing the organization’s journey from a student-led initiative to a nonprofit advancing education for Afghan girls abroad and refugee youth in Sacramento County.

pressItems.title: UC Davis student founds School of Freedom to educate Afghan girls amid Taliban ban
pressItems.description: In-depth coverage of Walid Jailani's mission to provide education to Afghan girls through The School of Freedom.

pressItems.title: Partnership with AEWA
pressItems.description: School of Freedom is proud to announce our partnership with the Alliance for the Education of Women in Afghanistan (AEWA).

pressItems.title: Partnership with Elk Grove Unified School District
pressItems.description: School of Freedom is excited to announce our partnership with Elk Grove Unified School District to offer remote volunteering opportunities in tutoring, mentorship, and college preparation support, mainly for refugee and newcomer kids. If interested, please click the link and join us.

pressItems.title: Afghan-American Community Organization’s 10th Annual Conference
pressItems.description: It was a pleasure to facilitate at the Afghan-American Community Organization’s 10th Annual Conference, sharing insights on bridging educational gaps for Afghan girls and refugee youth.

programHighlights.title: A Light in the Dark: How Our Secret Schools are Thriving
programHighlights.author: Walid Jailani, CEO
programHighlights.description: Despite the immense challenges, our network of underground schools in Afghanistan continues to grow. Discover how our brave teachers and resilient students are keeping the flame of education alive...

programHighlights.title: Preparing for WASC Accreditation: Our Next Step Forward
programHighlights.author: Walid Jailani, CEO
programHighlights.description: We are excited to share that School of Freedom will soon be filing for WASC accreditation. This milestone reflects our commitment to providing high-quality education and expanding opportunities for our students as we continue to grow.

programHighlights.title: Leadership Spotlight: Sadia Haidari’s Vision for Education in Afghanistan
programHighlights.author: Robina Haqiqi, Vice President
programHighlights.description: We are proud to spotlight Sadia Haidari, Principal and Founding Member of the School of Freedom Afghanistan Program. As a dedicated leader, Sadia has played a foundational role in building and sustaining educational opportunities for students. Her commitment to empowering education and supporting young learners reflects her strong vision for a brighter future. Through her leadership, she continues to inspire both students and educators, helping create a lasting impact in her community.

programHighlights.title: Community Spotlight: Nouman Siddiqi’s Impact Through Technology and Advocacy
programHighlights.author: Walid Jailani, CEO
programHighlights.description: Meet Nouman Siddiqi, whose work bridges technology and social impact. With a background in computer applications, Nouman has been actively involved in supporting girls’ education in Afghanistan. Through his efforts, he continues to raise awareness about gender equality and the importance of education in empowering future generations.

programHighlights.title: Leadership Spotlight: Omar Alhuniti Leading the Davis Initiative
programHighlights.author: Walid Jailani, CEO
programHighlights.description: We are proud to spotlight Omar Alhuniti, our Previous Outreach Coordinator and the new Director of the School of Freedom Davis Initiative. With a strong background in nonprofit work, student support, and community engagement, Omar has been instrumental in building trust and meaningful connections within our community. Rooted in his Palestinian heritage, he brings a deep understanding of displacement and resilience, shaping his passion for service and advocacy. As a UC Davis student pursuing a career in healthcare, Omar leads with compassion, accountability, and vision. In his new role, he is focused on expanding School of Freedom’s presence on the UC Davis campus, creating more impactful events, and building a stronger, more connected community. His leadership marks an exciting step toward a bigger and more influential future for School of Freedom in Davis.

programHighlights.title: Community Spotlight: Ghezal Karimi’s Commitment to Mental Health and Education
programHighlights.author: Robina Haqiqi, Vice President
programHighlights.description: We are proud to spotlight Ghezal Karimi, a recent UC Davis graduate with a degree in Psychology and Economics and an incoming doctoral student in Clinical Psychology at Alliant International University. Ghezal has been actively involved in mental health education and advocacy, using her knowledge to support and uplift others. As an Afghan-American immigrant, she is deeply passionate about giving back to her community. Through her work with the School of Freedom, she plays an important role in supporting Afghan girls in accessing education and building a brighter future. Her dedication, compassion, and commitment to service continue to inspire those around her.

programHighlights.title: Leadership Spotlight: Sahar Sadeed Supporting Education in Afghanistan
programHighlights.author: Robina Haqiqi, Vice President
programHighlights.description: We are proud to spotlight Sahar Sadeed, a dedicated educator and Vice Principal in Afghanistan. As a committed member of the School of Freedom team, Sahar plays a vital role in empowering students and strengthening access to education. Her leadership, passion, and unwavering support for students reflect her deep belief in the power of education to transform lives. Through her work, she continues to inspire and uplift the next generation, helping create opportunities for growth and success in her community.

programHighlights.title: Student Spotlight: Fatima's Journey to Becoming a Coder
programHighlights.author: Sadia Haidari, Principal
programHighlights.description: Meet Fatima, a recent graduate who defied the odds to learn coding through our online program. Today, she's working as a junior developer and inspiring her entire community.
